channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Augusta chairman confident Masters will go on as club focuses on community recovery from Helene
item.description: Augusta National Chairman Fred Ridley said Thursday he was confident the Masters would be held as scheduled in April as the club directs its attention and resources to helping the area recover from devastating damage from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Shaboozey to perform prior to Utah Hockey Club's first home game
item.description: The National Hockey League has announced that the creator of the smash hit "A Bar Song (Tipsy)," Shaboozey will perform outside of the Delta Center ahead of the Utah Hockey Club's first regular season game.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: Patrick Mahomes and Bobby Witt Jr. come from shared backgrounds. Both have KC teams chasing titles
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — They were the sons of big league ballplayers, the lazy summer days of their youth spent listening to stories about baseball.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Travis Kelce jokingly references ‘Second Amendment’ in defense of Jets player’s finger gun celebration
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce defended Allen Lazard this week after the Jets wideout was hit with a 15-yard penalty for a finger gun celebration after getting a first down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chiefs place Rashee Rice on injured reserve; severity of knee injury still unknown
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs are still waiting to determine the severity of Rashee Rice's injury, but it's enough for them to place him on injured reserve.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark a near-unanimous choice as WNBA's Rookie of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/WGN) — Caitlin Clark has been named the WNBA Rookie of the Year in a near-unanimous vote, giving the Indiana Fever back-to-back winners after Aliyah Boston won the honor last season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Teen pleads guilty in shooting death of Southern Miss cornerback MJ Daniels
item.description: HATTIESBURG, Miss. (AP) — One of four people charged in the shooting death of Southern Miss football player Marcus “MJ” Daniels Jr. has pleaded guilty to second-degree murder. Nytavion Shaw, 16, of Laurel, Mississippi, was charged as an adult.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Devils meet the Sabres in the Czech capital to open the NHL regular season
item.description: PRAGUE (AP) — Ondrej Palat has won the Stanley Cup twice. He's won a gold medal with the Czech Republic at the world championships.
item.dc:creator: KAREL JANICEK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Travis Kelce's best friend dishes on Taylor Swift's influence since two stars began dating
item.description: One of Travis Kelce's best friends talked to Page Six about Taylor Swift and her influence on him and around the group. The two started dating last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Messi, Inter Miami to open playoffs at home on Oct. 25. And it'll be shown live in Times Square
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Lionel Messi's first MLS Cup playoff match with Inter Miami will be on Oct. 25. And it'll be aired live on a massive screen spanning an entire block of New York's Times Square.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah State Volleyball cancels match against San Jose State, becomes fourth team to do so
item.description: The Utah State women's volleyball team has canceled their Oct. 23 match against San Jose State becoming the fourth team this year to do so, according to reports and tweets from state officials. This comes amid a swirling controversy over a SJSU player's gender.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: Picks of the Week: Washington, Cal and Arizona State well-positioned to cover (and win) in Week 6
item.description: Cal has several advantages against unbeaten Miami, including a familiarity with Canes coach Mario Cristobal.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: World ski body and UN weather agency team up to help winter sports plan for climate change
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Facing a long-time crisis in winter sports because of climate change, the International Ski and Snowboard Federation teamed up with the United Nations weather agency on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hall of Famer Susie Maxwell Berning, a 4-time major champion while raising 2 daughters, dies at 83
item.description: PALM SPRINGS, Calif. (AP) — Susie Maxwell Berning, the three-time U.S. Women's Open winner who was a pioneer as a mother while competing on the LPGA Tour, died Wednesday after a two-year battle with lung cancer. She was 83.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Higashioka's homer starts rally as Padres beat Braves 5-4 to sweep NL Wild Card Series
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Kyle Higashioka's solo homer started a five-run rally against an ailing Max Fried with two outs in the second inning, and the San Diego Padres held on to sweep the Atlanta Braves with a 5-4 victory in Game 2 of their NL Wild Card Series on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Witt gets another big hit to send the Royals into ALDS with a 2-game sweep of the Orioles
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Bobby Witt Jr. beat out an infield single to drive in the go-ahead run and send the Kansas City Royals into an AL Division Series with a 2-1 victory over the Baltimore Orioles on Wednesday for a two-game sweep of their Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Californians think this is the unofficial boundary between Northern and Southern California
item.description: There is no true defining line that represents the distinction between Northern and Southern California, but it’s still a widely popular debate. Some Californians believe the transition from SoCal to NorCal happens when sports fans cheer for the Giants and 49ers as opposed to the Dodgers and Rams.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bucs remain banged up but carry confidence into Thursday's Falcons clash
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are riding momentum into Thursday night’s NFC South clash with the Atlanta Falcons but so far, the short game week has presented many challenges.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NCAA March Madness returning to San Francisco
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- March Madness is returning to San Francisco, to Chase Center, specifically. On Wednesday, the NCAA announced that SF's Chase Center will host the 2028 Division I Men's Basketball West Regionals. The West Coast Conference will serve as the host.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Zach LaVine still a Chicago Bull (for now), says he's ready to do whatever teams asks of him this season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Zach LaVine insisted he is healthy and ready to do whatever the Chicago Bulls ask of him this season. Above all, he's ready to put the drama in the past.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Homer Glen native CM Punk reflects on wrestling's past, future on the eve of NXT's CW Network debut from Rosemont
item.description: On the eve of WWE NXT's debut on the CW Network, superstar CM Punk reflected on the professional wrestling industry's past, present, and future.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, WGN 9

item.title: Bears vs. Rams: Injuries, inactives and keys to Sunday's game at Soldier Field
item.description: Los Angeles Rams (1-2) at Chicago Bears (1-2) Sunday, Noon, FOX UPDATE: The Bears just released their list of inactives on X. Inactive for Sunday's game are WR Velus Jones Jr., FB Khari Blasingame, DL Dominique Robinson, DB Terrell Smith, OL Bill Murray and DL Zacch Pickens.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox look to break Tigers' home win streak
item.description: Chicago White Sox (39-121, fifth in the AL Central) vs. Detroit Tigers (86-74, second in the AL Central) Detroit; Saturday, 1:10 p.m. EDT PITCHING PROBABLES: White Sox: Sean Burke (0-0); Tigers: TBD BOTTOM LINE: The Detroit Tigers, on a five-game home winning streak, host the Chicago White Sox.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
